tenors.narrative: Prediction markets show minimal price movement by early April 2026, with a median of $71,593 (-0.3% from spot) across 99 markets. The 25th-75th percentile range of $70,000-$73,423 suggests modest consolidation, with downside risk to $68,000 (10th percentile) and modest upside to $75,222 (90th percentile).

By end of April 2026, markets price in a 6.4% appreciation to $76,389 median, with deeper conviction on the upside than downside. The interquartile range of $62,656-$79,625 reflects meaningful divergence in expectations, though the 90th percentile of $84,167 suggests tail risk skews higher during this period.

Year-end 2026 predictions show a stark bifurcated market with zero trading volume, indicating substantial uncertainty and model disagreement. The median of $48,846 implies -32% downside, yet the 90th percentile reaches $129,999, suggesting markets cannot consensus on a base case and are pricing tail scenarios rather than a tight distribution.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets expect WTI to decline 16.2% to a median of $80/bbl by April 2026, with a broad range reflecting significant uncertainty. The interquartile spread ($73-$87) suggests most forecasters anticipate a modest pullback, though the 90th percentile at $134 indicates tail risks of supply disruptions. With $7.3M in volume across 21 markets, this tenor has meaningful liquidity supporting the consensus bearish view.

By June 2026, prediction markets turn decidedly more bearish, pricing a 23.5% decline to $73/bbl median as the market prices in persistent oversupply or demand softness. The 25th percentile at $64 and 10th percentile at $56 suggest heightened downside risks, though the extreme 90th percentile jump to $162 points to lingering geopolitical tail risks. Lower volume ($900K across 15 markets) makes this tenor less reliable than the April contract.
